methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The pending tax to be swapped must never exceed the actual token balance held by the contract.
invariants.attack_scenario: If projectTaxPendingSwap exceeds the contract's balance, the autoswap mechanism will revert or miscalculate proportions, leading to a denial of service on transfers or incorrect tax distribution.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_tax_conservation() public view { assert(projectTaxPendingSwap <= balanceOf(address(this))); }

invariants.description: The total withdrawable amount across all active applications must be less than or equal to the factory's balance of the asset token.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the factory's asset token balance is less than the sum of withdrawable amounts, some proposers will not be able to withdraw their funds or execute their applications, leading to insolvency and loss of user funds.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_factory_solvency() public view { uint256 totalWithdrawable; for(uint i=0; i<nextId; i++) { totalWithdrawable += applications[i].withdrawableAmount; } assert(totalWithdrawable <= IERC20(assetToken).balanceOf(address(this))); }

invariants.description: The total supply of the veToken must be exactly equal to the balance of the underlying staking asset held by the contract.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the contract holds fewer staking assets than the veToken total supply, users will not be able to withdraw their staked tokens, leading to a bank run and loss of funds.

invariants.description: The founder's balance cannot drop below the initial lock amount before the maturity timestamp.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the founder can withdraw their initial lock before maturity, it violates the trust assumption and economic security of the agent, potentially allowing a rug pull of the initial liquidity.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_founder_lock() public view { if (block.timestamp < matureAt) { assert(balanceOf(founder) >= initialLock); } }

invariants.description: Project tax rates can only ever decrease, protecting users from sudden fee hikes.
invariants.attack_scenario: If an admin can arbitrarily increase the tax rate, they could front-run user trades with a 100% tax rate, effectively stealing the transfer amount. The code's comments state it should only decrease, but lacks enforcement.
invariants.solidity_property: function rule_tax_rate_monotonicity(uint16 newBuy, uint16 newSell) public { uint16 oldBuy = projectBuyTaxBasisPoints; uint16 oldSell = projectSellTaxBasisPoints; setProjectTaxRates(newBuy, newSell); assert(projectBuyTaxBasisPoints <= oldBuy && projectSellTaxBasisPoints <= oldSell); }

invariants.description: The Uniswap pair cannot receive any project tokens before the official initial liquidity is added by the contract.
invariants.attack_scenario: If an attacker can transfer tokens to the pair before initialization, they can manipulate the initial price and steal the initial liquidity provided by the protocol.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_initial_liquidity_protection() public view { if (fundedDate == 0) { assert(balanceOf(uniswapV2Pair) == 0); } }
